workflow_details.description: Add information that will help to differentiate this workflow from another.
workflow_details.title: Set workflow details

data_source.description: Select one of your configured data sources.

time_range.description: Choose the period of time to analyze.
time_range.title: Define analysis time range

Subject Group.description: Choose subject group to generate collar voltage charts and overall speedmap

EarthRanger Spatial Features.description: Optionally overlay EarthRanger spatial feature layers (e.g. protected areas, reporting zones) on the animated map. Enable the toggle to turn this on.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Include this optional EarthRanger-dependent branch of the workflow. When off, the client is replaced with a skip sentinel so this step (and anything built from its return value) is skipped entirely.

group_by.title: Group By
group_by.description: Column used to group features in the map legend e.g. 'Feature Type' shows one legend entry per feature type.

legend_title.title: Legend Title
legend_title.description: Label shown in the map legend e.g. 'Park Boundary'.

trajectory_segment_filter.description: Filter track data by setting limits on track segment length, duration, and speed. Segments outside these bounds are removed, reducing noise and to focus on meaningful movement patterns.

exaggeration.title: Exaggeration
exaggeration.description: Vertical exaggeration factor. 1.0 = true scale, 2.0 = 2x heights.

elevation_data.title: Elevation Data
elevation_data.description: URL template (or single image) for the RGB-encoded elevation tiles.

texture.title: Texture
texture.description: URL template for tiles draped over the terrain.

pitch.title: Pitch
pitch.description: Camera tilt in degrees (0 = top-down, 90 = horizon). 45° gives a natural 3-D perspective over terrain.

bearing.title: Bearing
bearing.description: Camera compass heading in degrees (0 = north, 90 = east, -90 = west). Rotates the map so a different cardinal direction faces up.

head_layer.title: Head Layer
head_layer.description: 3D glTF/GLB head model settings. Enable with the 'enabled' checkbox.

Video Creation.description: Optionally render the animated map as a video file. Enable the toggle to turn on video creation.

MepFeatureIdQuery.description: Load a single spatial feature by its EarthRanger UUID.

feature_id.description: UUID of a specific spatial feature available on EarthRanger.
feature_id.title: Feature Id

style.description: Optional: Override how EarthRanger spatial features are rendered on the map. If not specified, features will use their native EarthRanger colours and styling.
style.title: Style

MepFeatureIdQuery.title: Feature ID

MepFeatureSetQuery.description: Load all features from a named EarthRanger featureset.

featureset_name.description: Display name of the featureset exactly as it appears in EarthRanger e.g. 'Boundaries'. Leave blank to load nothing (e.g. while this branch of the workflow is disabled).
featureset_name.title: Featureset Name

MepFeatureSetQuery.title: Feature Set

MepFeatureTypeQuery.description: Load all features of a given type across EarthRanger featuresets.

feature_type.description: Feature type name as shown in EarthRanger e.g. 'Conservancy'.
feature_type.title: Feature Type

style.description: Optional: Override how EarthRanger spatial features are rendered on the map. If not specified, features will use their native EarthRanger colours and styling.
style.title: Style

MepFeatureTypeQuery.title: Feature Type

polygon.description: Polygon styling. Add one entry to override ER native colours.
polygon.title: Polygon

line.description: Line styling. Add one entry to override ER native colours.
line.title: Line

point.description: Point and icon marker styling. Add one entry to override ER native colours.
point.title: Point

color.description: Line hex colour(s) e.g. ['#E63946']. Cycles across rows.
color.title: Color

MepNoQuery.description: Load nothing (e.g. while this branch of the workflow is disabled).
MepNoQuery.title: None

color.description: Fill hex colour(s). For SVG icons this tints the marker. Cycles across rows.
color.title: Color

size.description: Point radius / icon size in pixels. Leave empty to use the size set in EarthRanger.
size.title: Size

fill_color.description: Fill hex colour(s) e.g. ['#FFA500']. Cycles across rows.
fill_color.title: Fill Color

ScenegraphLayerDefinition.description:
An animated 3D head built from a glTF/GLB model (deck.gl ScenegraphLayer).

Create one with create_scenegraph_layer() and pass it to
draw_animated_map(head_layer=...). Its position and heading are driven per-frame
from the TripsLayer, so it follows each subject's current location. `glb` accepts
an http(s) URL, a `data:` URI, or a local file path (read and embedded as a data
URI). None -> bundled default (the elephant model). If the ScenegraphLayer
constructor or the glTF loader can't be resolved at runtime, the head silently
falls back to the flat ScatterplotLayer dot.
See https://deck.gl/docs/api-reference/mesh-layers/scenegraph-layer for more info.

enabled.description: Enable the 3D head model. When off, subjects render as flat dots.
enabled.title: Enabled

glb.description: GLB source: an http(s) URL, a data: URI, or a local file path. None -> bundled default model (elephant).
glb.title: Glb

size_scale.description: ScenegraphLayer sizeScale. Tune to your scene.
size_scale.title: Size Scale

size_min_pixels.description: Clamp the on-screen model to at least this many pixels so it stays visible when zoomed out.
size_min_pixels.title: Size Min Pixels

size_max_pixels.description: Optional upper clamp on the model's on-screen size in pixels.
size_max_pixels.title: Size Max Pixels

face_heading.description: Rotate the model to face its direction of travel.
face_heading.title: Face Heading

yaw_offset.description: Degrees added to the computed heading so the model's nose aligns with travel. Model-dependent; tweak if your model faces sideways.
yaw_offset.title: Yaw Offset

model_pitch.description: Tilt of the MODEL itself (deg), independent of the camera. Use to correct a model authored nose-up/down; NOT the view pitch.
model_pitch.title: Model Pitch

model_roll.description: Bank of the MODEL itself (deg), independent of the camera.
model_roll.title: Model Roll

smooth_samples.description: Heading/slope smoothing window in track fixes (+/- N). Higher = smoother orientation but more lag; 0 = raw single segment.
smooth_samples.title: Smooth Samples

terrain_pitch.description: Tilt the model to the terrain slope (from each fix's z) so it noses up on climbs / down on descents. OFF by default -> stays upright; enable only for steady, climbing tracks (it can tip near-stationary subjects).
terrain_pitch.title: Terrain Pitch

terrain_pitch_scale.description: Sign/strength of terrain pitch. Set -1.0 to flip if the model tilts the wrong way; <1 to soften. (Tilt is also capped at +/-20deg.)
terrain_pitch_scale.title: Terrain Pitch Scale

use_track_color.description: Colour the model with each subject's track colour. False -> use `tint` (or the model's own materials). Note: the colour multiplies the model's material, so it reads truest with a light/neutral glb and flat lighting (pbr_lighting=False).
use_track_color.title: Use Track Color

ScenegraphLayerDefinition.title: ScenegraphLayerDefinition

CinematicCamera.description:
Camera does a smooth fly-through: leads the subject, banks its bearing,
and optionally flies in from altitude at the start.

Faithful to the Mapbox "cinematic route" technique, expressed in deck.gl's
MapView.
CinematicCamera.title: CinematicCamera

CustomResolution.description: Output video resolution at an exact width/height.
CustomResolution.title: Custom

auto.description: Match the animation's own playback length. Uncheck to set a fixed duration.
auto.title: Auto

FitCamera.description:
Camera zooms to keep every point visited so far in frame.

Zoom is always computed from the visited bounds (there's no zoom
override); pitch and bearing can still be fixed.
FitCamera.title: FitCamera

Follow3DCamera.description:
Camera tracks a subject (or the group) in a tilted, 3D chase-cam view.

Like FollowCamera, but pitched by default and able to rotate its heading
to match the tracked subject's direction of travel (heading_lock).
Follow3DCamera.title: Follow3DCamera

FollowCamera.description:
Camera tracks a subject (or the group) in a flat, top-down-ish view.

Longitude/latitude follow the tracked subject(s) every frame; pitch stays
level (0) unless overridden and bearing stays fixed. For a tilted, 3D
chase-cam that can also rotate with the subject's heading, use
Follow3DCamera instead.
FollowCamera.title: FollowCamera

KeyframesCamera.description:
Camera flies through a set of waypoints while the data animates.

Pick a `source`: upload a waypoint file (KeyframesFromFile) or auto-derive a
path by following a subject (KeyframesFromSubject) -- see also the
derive_camera_keyframes task, which does the same derivation as an explicit,
inspectable/editable list of keyframes. Alternatively, supply your own
``keyframes`` (a list of CameraKeyframe) programmatically -- these can be
placed anywhere and are not tied to any subject's track, and take priority
over `source` when non-empty.

source.description: How to build the camera path when `keyframes` is empty: upload a file, or auto-derive one by following a subject. Ignored when `keyframes` is provided directly.
source.title: Source

keyframe_easing.description: How the camera moves between keyframes: 'smooth' eases in/out of each waypoint, 'linear' moves at constant speed, 'spline' curves through waypoints (Catmull-Rom) without pausing at them.
keyframe_easing.title: Keyframe Easing

zoom.description: Zoom applied to every auto-derived keyframe. None -> the scene's initial zoom. Not user-configurable; set via a workflow's spec.yaml if a non-default value is needed.
zoom.title: Zoom

pitch.description: Pitch applied to every auto-derived keyframe. None -> the scene's initial pitch. Not user-configurable; set via a workflow's spec.yaml if a non-default value is needed.
pitch.title: Pitch

bearing.description: Bearing applied to every auto-derived keyframe. None -> the scene's initial bearing. Not user-configurable; set via a workflow's spec.yaml if a non-default value is needed.
bearing.title: Bearing

KeyframesCamera.title: KeyframesCamera

KeyframesFromFile.description: Camera path loaded from an uploaded waypoint file.

keyframes_file.description: Path to an uploaded keyframe file: a .json list of {lon, lat, t?, zoom?, pitch?, bearing?} objects, a .geojson of Point features (extras read from properties), or a .csv/.tsv with lon/lat columns.
keyframes_file.title: Keyframes File

KeyframesFromFile.title: KeyframesFromFile

KeyframesFromSubject.description: Camera path auto-derived from the animated data by following one subject (or the group).

subject.description: Which subject to follow -- a value from the 'name' (or 'groupby_col') column, a positional index (as digits, e.g. '2'), 'all' for the group's mean position, or empty for the longest-running track.
subject.title: Subject

KeyframesFromSubject.title: KeyframesFromSubject

OrbitCamera.description:
Camera circles the scene's centroid at a constant tilt.

Not tied to any subject -- it orbits the mean position of every visited
point in the scene.
OrbitCamera.title: OrbitCamera

PresetResolution.description: Output video resolution from a common preset.
PresetResolution.title: Preset

StaticCamera.description:
Camera holds the scene's initial view for the whole clip.

No motion, no overrides -- whatever view the map/animation was authored
with is what renders. Pick one of the other camera types for movement.
StaticCamera.title: StaticCamera
